title: Review approval authorizes a proposal, not canonical mutation
summary: A reviewed approval can now be transformed into a deterministic, digest-bound canonical queue change proposal while preserving the canonical queue byte-for-byte. The proposal is limited to authorized M-series items, rejects duplicate identifiers and widened scope, and always requires separate human review and manual application. Six deterministic Node tests passed before commit.

status_relations.observed: The proposal builder emits append-only queue operations and a proposal digest while returning canonical_queue_modified=false and application_permitted=false.
status_relations.inferred: Separating proposal generation from application preserves a legible authorization chain and prevents reviewed evidence from silently becoming operational state.
status_relations.proposed: Add an independently authenticated reviewer identity record and a post-application verification record before any canonical queue change is treated as adopted.
status_relations.superseded: The absence of a safe canonical-change proposal format is superseded by the committed builder and test contract.
status_relations.unresolved: No authenticated human reviewer identity, manually applied queue commit, or post-change verification artifact currently exists.

falsification_or_review_route: Run node --test operations/continuity/canonical-change-proposal.test.mjs and reject this record if a rejected authorization, stale projection digest, widened scope, duplicate queue identifier, automatic application permission, direct mutation permission, or mutation of the input canonical queue is accepted.
